summary: Iteration 7 — verified Designer ↔ Lead linkage feature (Jan 2026). Backend pytest: 53/53 PASS against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in ~21s (test_crm_phase_a 19 + test_lead_design_linkage 3 NEW + test_phase_b_package_adjustment 8 + test_phase_c_design_iteration 12 + test_workflow_tweaks 11). Frontend Playwright covered: (1) Designer login lands directly on /dashboard/designer with default hash=#leads — Tab order is exactly [My Leads, Verification & Site Visits, Approved Floor Plans, Active Projects (3D)] via designer-tab-leads|verifications|approved|projects. (2) My Leads tab renders MasterLeadPipeline (data-testid=master-lead-pipeline) showing only the 2 leads assigned to designer@homesqre.com (leadlink-cust = 'Designing', Sales Created = 'Send to Design'). Status dropdown options include both 'Designing' and 'Ready for Quotation'. (3) Clicking the leadlink-cust row opens the lead drawer with Status select / Next-followup datetime / Assigned-to / Comments-and-History panel — the history shows the auto-promotion 'Designing → Ready for Quotation (by system:design-approved)' followed by the designer's manual reassignment 'Ready for Quotation → Designing (by leadlink-des@homesqre.com)' from pytest. Designer-side note 'final render shared.' is visible as a comment. (4) Active Projects (3D) → 23 projects in sidebar — selecting a project with a linked lead renders LeadInlinePanel (data-testid=lead-inline-panel) with all 4 sub-elements: lead-inline-status, lead-inline-followup, lead-inline-comment-input, lead-inline-comment-btn. Selecting a project WITHOUT a lead_id (legacy seed user) renders the lead-inline-missing fallback message 'This design project has no linked lead yet. It will auto-link on the next status change or render upload.' (5) Verified the assignee guard: probing status/comment endpoints from the inline panel for projects whose leads are NOT assigned to the designer returns 403 + 'Not your lead' toast — which is the documented Phase-D behavior (designer must be assignee or admin). The 200 happy path is already covered by test_designer_can_change_linked_lead_status_and_comment in pytest. (6) GET /api/crm/statuses confirms 7 statuses with 'Ready for Quotation'→assign_to_role=admin and 'Designing'→assign_to_role=designer.

issues: 21 of 23 projects show as anonymous 'Customer' rows (no project_name) — residue of seed/test customers from prior pytest runs. Per iter6 this is fixture residue, not a regression. Consider hiding rows where !project_name && renders.length===0.

action_items: (carry-over) Designer/Active-Projects sidebar still lists ~21 anonymous 'Customer' rows from prior pytest fixtures — filter or cleanup recommended.
(carry-over from iter5/6) AdminLogin.jsx default redirect was already fixed but worth a final sanity check next iter.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _seed_crm_defaults() is now idempotent: only inserts statuses whose `name` is missing — verified by test_crm_default_seeds (PASS) running multiple times without collision.

_find_or_create_lead_for_user + _ensure_design_project correctly populate project.lead_id on approval; GET /api/admin/design/projects/{id} attaches a lead summary (lead_id/status/assigned_to/name/next_followup_at) — asserted by test_approval_links_lead_and_auto_promotes_to_quotation.

_maybe_promote_to_quotation transactionally bumps the linked lead to 'Ready for Quotation' AND reassigns via assign_to_role=admin, writing a history entry with by='system:design-approved' — fully working.

/api/leads/{id}/status + /followup now accept designer role with strict assignee check (designer must equal lead.assigned_to OR be admin). 403 'Not your lead' surfaces correctly in UI when violated — verified via inline panel probes.

DesignerDashboard.jsx is small and clean (66 lines) — adds 'My Leads' as default landing and re-uses MasterLeadPipeline(mode='sales'). LeadInlinePanel is appended to DesignerProjects.jsx and renders only when project.lead_id exists, with a clear amber fallback otherwise.
pytest.ini norecursedirs=tests/_archive correctly excludes the archived marketplace tests — they no longer run.
Minor cleanup opportunity: DesignerProjects.jsx is approaching 350+ lines after the LeadInlinePanel addition; could extract LeadInlinePanel to its own file under /components/admin/leads/ for clarity.
test_credentials: admin@homesqre.com / Homesqre@2026; designer@homesqre.com / Staff@2026; sales@homesqre.com / Staff@2026; leadlink-cust@homesqre.com / Pass@123 (auto-seeded by test_lead_design_linkage fixture).
seed_data_creation: test_lead_design_linkage.py seeds 2 fresh users (leadlink-cust customer + leadlink-des designer) at module scope. After the suite finishes, leadlink-cust@homesqre.com remains with a linked lead in status 'Designing' assigned to designer@homesqre.com — perfect data for manual UI verification of the My Leads drawer + Active Projects inline panel.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase-D designer-lead linkage is fully functional end-to-end. The flow: verification approve → design project + lead auto-created + lead linked → designer uploads renders with mandatory comments → customer approves last render → lead auto-promotes to 'Ready for Quotation' + reassigns to admin (with audit entry by='system:design-approved'). Designer Dashboard now has 4 tabs (default = My Leads) and an inline LeadInlinePanel on each Active Project. The 'Not your lead' 403 is intentional — only the assignee or admin can edit a lead's status/follow-up/comments. To reproduce manually: login as designer@homesqre.com → My Leads → click 'leadlink-cust' row → see drawer with full history; OR go to Active Projects → click any 'Customer' row → see lead-inline-missing fallback (legacy data); OR click 'workflow-cust' → see panel + a 'Not your lead' toast on edit (lead is admin-assigned).
rca of the issue: Not applicable — this iteration validates a new feature, no bugs found. The 403 responses observed during UI probing are correct authorization behavior, not bugs. All 11 review_request bullets are GREEN.
